source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / database ops domain
description: Encapsulated knowledge for the database domain: risk taxonomy, harm priors, common adversarial patterns, and safe action criteria.

adversarial_patterns: SQL framed as 'optimization' but contains DROP/TRUNCATE
Migration script that includes irreversible data deletion
safe_criteria: Read-only queries with no side effects
Migrations with down-migration tested in staging

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / medical domain
description: Medical domain: highest harm prior. All writes require expert verification. Regulatory scope: HIPAA, FDA, local medical law.

adversarial_patterns: AI framed as 'decision support' when actually executing medication orders
Bulk patient record modification framed as data cleanup
safe_criteria: Read-only retrieval of non-PII medical data
De-identified research queries with IRB approval
payload.aromer_rule: Any action_type containing 'write', 'prescribe', 'administer' or 'modify' in medical domain → escalate regardless of trust_score

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / infrastructure domain
description: Infrastructure domain: network, cloud, server ops. Highly context-dependent — staging actions may be low risk, prod is high/critical.

safe_criteria: Config changes in sandbox with documented rollback
Deployments with feature flags and staged rollout
adversarial_patterns: Security bypass framed as 'temporary' config change
Resource limit removal framed as 'performance optimization'

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / agentic action domain
description: Actions taken by autonomous agents requesting REMORA governance. Key challenge: agents can be deceived, misconfigured, or adversarially prompted.

payload.trust_adjustment: Agent trust starts 0.10 lower than human requester trust. Agents can earn trust through verified history.
escalation_triggers: Agent claiming emergency bypass of governance
Agent delegating to sub-agent without AROMER coverage
safe_criteria: Agent operates within predefined tool manifest
Agent has human-in-the-loop at defined checkpoints
Agent actions are auditable and replayable
Agent trust built through verified episodic history
payload.aromer_rule: Nested agent governance (agent-in-agent): each level must have REMORA coverage. No autonomy laundering.

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / financial domain
description: Financial domain: high regulatory exposure. Actions affecting monetary transfers, pricing, billing, or financial reporting require elevated verification.

adversarial_patterns: Billing rule modification framed as 'bug fix'
safe_criteria: Single transfers below org-defined threshold with dual approval
Pricing changes in staging with A/B test evidence
